title: The Token Cost Problem
concept: Deploying LLM apps at scale generates high token costs; optimizing prompt size and model routing is essential to keep application unit economics viable.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary factor that determines the cost of using a cloud LLM API?

options.text: The number of words (tokens) sent and received
The time of day the app is used
The internet speed of the user

exercises.explain: Unlike traditional web servers where serving a page costs next to nothing, LLMs charge per word fragment (token) processed, meaning more text equals more real money spent.

cards.title: The Sticker Shock
cards.body: Building a cool AI prototype is cheap and fun! But what happens when 100,000 users start using it every day? Suddenly, your cloud bill goes from $5 to $5,000. Let's learn why AI scales differently than normal software.

cards.title: What is the Token Cost Problem?
cards.body: The Token Cost Problem is the financial challenge of running AI apps at scale. Because cloud providers charge you for every single token (word fragment) processed and generated, long prompts and heavy user traffic can quickly create massive, unsustainable bills.

cards.title: The Loaded Truck
cards.body: Imagine renting a moving truck where you are charged by the exact weight of every single item you pack. If you load it with heavy, unnecessary junk, your bill skyrockets. In the AI world, sending massive, wordy prompts to a premium LLM is like packing that heavy truck—you pay for every extra word (token) you send and receive!

cards.title: Trimming the Fat
cards.body: Imagine a customer support bot. If you feed the bot a giant 10-page user manual with every single customer question, you pay for thousands of tokens on every message. To save money, you can use a search system to only feed the bot the relevant 1-paragraph answer, cutting the token cost by 95%!

cards.title: The 'Biggest is Best' Myth
cards.body: People often think that to get the best AI app, you must always route every single request to the most powerful model available. In reality, doing this will bankrupt your app! Using smaller, specialized models for simple tasks is often just as good and costs a fraction of the price.

cards.title: Cost Control Comparison
cards.body: Comparing how we handle prompt design and model selection makes a huge difference in the monthly budget of a scaling startup.

right.text: Uses short, targeted prompt templates and routes easy queries to cheap models.

left.text: Sends huge, unoptimized documents to the premium model for every simple query.

exercises.prompt: Why do LLM application costs scale up so quickly compared to traditional software?

options.text: Hosting databases is the only expensive part of AI apps.
You pay for every input and output token, making high traffic very expensive.
AI models run entirely for free once they are trained.
Users pay the cloud providers directly for their usage.

exercises.explain: In the cloud, you are billed directly for the computational work of reading and writing text (tokens). If your app becomes highly popular, your bill grows with every single message sent.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Routing simple tasks to smaller models is a valid way to reduce token costs.
exercises.explain: Different tasks require different levels of intelligence. Simple tasks like categorization can be done by cheaper models, saving you money.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete this cost-saving rule:
exercises.sentence: To lower your AI bill, you must reduce the number of processed _____ per request.
exercises.explain: LLMs charge based on 'tokens' (word pieces). If you send less text to the model, you process fewer tokens and save money.

exercises.prompt: Which AI system design shows smart token cost optimization?

options.text: AI A: 'I send every single spelling correction request to the most advanced GPT model.'
AI B: 'I use a cheap, lightweight model to detect the user's language before using a larger model for translation.'
AI C: 'I include our entire company history in every API request just in case the user asks about it.'

exercises.explain: Routing simple tasks like sorting or greeting to a fast, cheap model saves premium model tokens for the complex reasoning tasks.

exercises.prompt: You are launching a customer support chatbot that expects millions of users daily. Which strategy best solves the Token Cost Problem to keep your business profitable?

options.text: Send every user query to the largest, most expensive model to ensure quality.
Shorten prompt templates, cache common answers, and route simple questions to smaller models.
Ask users to write much longer prompts so the model doesn't have to guess.
Avoid using system prompts entirely and process all data on local user devices.

exercises.explain: To keep an app financially viable at scale, you must reduce unnecessary tokens by shortening prompts, caching repeat data, and sending simple requests to smaller, cheaper models while reserving premium models for hard tasks.
